title: Kimi Code is good at video reasoning with Kimi K2.6 🎬
summary: Kimi Code is a video reasoning tool built on Kimi K2.6 that accepts reference videos and generates .cube LUT (color lookup table) files based on visual queries about colors, shots, or visual style. Users can drag in video references and request specific visual attributes to generate ready-to-use color grading outputs.
writeup: The video demonstrates Kimi Code, a video reasoning tool built on Kimi K2.6 that analyzes reference videos and produces .cube LUT files for color grading. Users drag in video references and ask about colors, shots, or visual style, and the tool returns ready-to-use color lookup tables matching the requested visual attributes. This connects model reasoning over video to a concrete production output, turning visual queries into deliverable grading files.
